article_title: GCC 16 Nears Release With C++26 Reflection and Contracts, Algol 68 Frontend, and C++20 as the New Default

content_preview.title: GCC 16 Nears Release With C++26 Reflection and Contracts, Algol 68 Frontend, and C++20 as the New Default
content_preview.summary: The GNU Compiler Collection's next major release enters its final regression-fixing phase with early C++26 support, a new Algol 68 frontend, AMD Zen 6 targets, and a long-awaited shift to C++20 as the default C++ dialect.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GCC 16, the next major release of the GNU Compiler Collection, is approaching its release candidate milestone after entering Stage 4 development in January 2026. The release brings a notable convergence of modernization efforts: early support for the newly finalized C++26 standard (including compile-time reflection and contracts), the addition of Algol 68 as a supported language, a default dialect shift to C++20, and hardware target expansions covering AMD Zen 6 and Intel's latest p...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent developer tools article with rich technical detail covering C++26 features, compiler changes, and hardware targets. Great use of official GCC sources.
editor_notes.source_verification: GCC changes.html confirms C++26 reflection (P2996R13), contracts (P2900R14), expansion statements, constexpr exceptions/virtual inheritance, Algol 68 ga68, AMD znver6, Intel Wildcat/Nova Lake, gfx942. The Register confirms C++26 approval March 28 Croydon, 114-12-3 vote, Sutter reflection quote, Stroustrup contracts opposition. All verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against primary sources. Sutter and Stroustrup quotes confirmed verbatim by The Register.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, technically precise, with appropriate developer audience awareness.
editor_notes.originality: No prior GCC 16 or C++26 coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding developer tools article with primary source verification. Approved.
